ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANTS' WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC AND REJUVENATION, INC DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 12TH CAUSE OF ACTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC., AND REJUVENATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS A MATTER OF LAW, REIMBURSEMENT OWED FOR ADVANCE PAYMENT OF PARTY SUPPLIES ARE NOT "WAGES" AS THAT TERM IS USED IN SECTIONS 200 THROUGH 203 OF THE LABOR CODE. THIS COURT AGREES WITH THE DECISION AND ANALYSIS OF THE JUDGE MENDEZ IN NELSON V. DOLLAR TREE STORES, INC. (E.D. CA 2011) 2011 WL 3568498. THE COURT ALSO TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF AND GIVES CONSIDERABLE DEFERENCE TO SECTION 4.3.4 OF THE DLSE ENFORCEMENT POLICIES AND INTERPRETATIONS MANUAL WHICH STATES THAT THE TERM "WAGES" DOES NOT INCLUDE EXPENSES. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2017 Set For Trial On Oct-02-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Yosef Peretz of the law offices of Peretz & Associates (415.483.7717) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Alexandra Pavlidakis of the law offices of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (650.614.7372) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC., and REJUVENATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION in the First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. As a matter of law, reimbursement owed for advance payment of party supplies are not "wages" as that term is used in sections 200 through 203 of the Labor Code. This court agrees with the decision and analysis of the Judge Mendez in Nelson v. Dollar Tree Stores, Inc. (E.D. CA 2011) 2011 WL 3568498. The court also takes judicial notice of and gives considerable deference to section 4.3.4 of the DLSE Enforcement Policies and Interpretations Manual which states that the term "wages" does not include expenses. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
